diagrams.title: Cloud Service Models Hierarchy
diagrams.description: A layered pyramid diagram showing the three cloud service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) from bottom to top, illustrating how each builds upon the previous layer and the increasing abstraction level.
elements: Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS) layer at the bottom
Platform as a Service (PaaS) layer in the middle
Software as a Service (SaaS) layer at the top
On-premises infrastructure layer below IaaS for comparison
Arrow or connection lines showing dependencies between layers
Label indicating each model manages different components
diagrams.purpose: Helps learners visualize the hierarchical relationship between service models and understand how they build upon each other

diagrams.title: Shared Responsibility Matrix
diagrams.description: A comparative diagram showing the division of responsibilities between the cloud provider and customer for each service model, using a stacked or matrix layout.
elements: Three horizontal sections for IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Customer responsibility zone in each section
Provider responsibility zone in each section
Labels showing what each party manages (hardware, OS, runtime, data, applications)
Progression arrow showing decreasing customer responsibility from IaaS to SaaS
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the shared responsibility model and helps users understand what they manage versus what the provider manages in each service model

diagrams.title: IaaS PaaS SaaS Comparison Overview
diagrams.description: A comprehensive visual comparison showing examples, use cases, and characteristics of each service model side by side.
elements: Three vertical columns for IaaS, PaaS, SaaS
Real-world examples for each (AWS EC2, Heroku, Gmail)
Icon or visual representing infrastructure, platform, software
Level of control indicator (high to low)
Level of management indicator (low to high)
Common use cases listed for each model
diagrams.purpose: Provides a quick reference for understanding differences between models, including practical examples and when to use each service type
